title: Luscious Middle Eastern Date Ouzi
description: A briny, velvety Middle Eastern stew of dates and anchovy that ends with a swirl of pistachio ice cream, perfect for a monsoon-inspired dessert.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in chopped fennel and cherry tomatoes, cook until tomatoes soften, about 5 minutes.
Add chopped dates, anchovy fillets, ground cumin, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt; cook for 3 minutes, letting flavors meld.
Pour in 1 cup of water, bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and let cook for 15 minutes until dates are tender and sauce thickens.
Remove from heat and stir in a spoonful of pistachio ice cream, letting it melt into the warm sauce for a velvety finish.
Serve the warm stew over a bed of cooked millet or couscous, spooning extra sauce on top.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves and a final swirl of pistachio ice cream before serving.
